blocks.body: Future Forms — will, be going to, Future Continuous, and Future Perfect for decisions, plans, predictions, actions in progress, and completion before a future point.

{ "title": "What to pay attention to", "items": [ "If the decision arose right now, we usually choose will.", "If the plan was before the moment of speech, we usually choose be going to.", "If it is an opinion or assumption without direct evidence, we often use will.", "If there is visible evidence now, we often use be going to.", "If it matters that the action will be in progress at a future moment, look to Future Continuous.", "If a completed result before a future point matters most, look to Future Perfect." ] }
